title: Miconia myrmecina
taxonomy: Miconia myrmecina is classified within the family Melastomataceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales and is native to the Americas, with a diverse range of species found in Central and South America. Within the family Melastomataceae, Miconia myrmecina is placed within the genus Miconia, which contains over 1,000 species of plants. The genus Miconia is characterized by its small to medium-sized trees or shrubs, often with showy flowers and a range of growth habits. The exact classification of Miconia myrmecina is further refined within the genus, with species often distinguished based on their unique characteristics such as leaf shape, flower color, and growth habit.

intro: Miconia myrmecina, also known as the ant plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, characterized by its unique ability to support colonies of ants on its leaves and stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Miconia myrmecina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Miconia myrmecina is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
